[Custom donation »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17819722/207587874/2147176479?refcode=em_fr_2020.06.10_SPK_b1_all&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjk1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZWIzMzhkNy01ZWFiLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DxAZ7M33yLY1UyMTPrmVDiWAW2qpAyI6E5lQURZRN9w=)
---

I know firsthand what it’s like to deal with the complexities of our healthcare system, and I know that it needs to change. **When my father got diagnosed with cancer, I dropped out of college to care for him -- and when he passed away, our medical bills from his care almost left us bankrupt.** Millions of Americans know what that's like, and I’m running on behalf of all of us so no one has to face that hopeless feeling anymore.

I also know, through my decades of experience in the Foreign Service, that we need to restore America’s standing as a global leader in climate and diplomacy. And my identity as a Texan, a state who knows firsthand what the pain of gun violence feels like, informs my plans to bring common-sense gun reforms to Washington.

**I wish my Republican opponents also believed in championing the causes of the people in our district; instead, they’re racing each other as far to the right as possible.**

One of them is a businesswoman pouring millions of dollars of her own money into divisive and extremist television ads in an attempt to buy this seat.

The other is a former sheriff more focused on writing extremist Facebook posts on his campaign account than doing the work to connect with the people of this district.

**What we need in Washington isn’t a representative who will fan the flames of division for their own gain, and neither Republican candidate is suited to meet the pressing needs of our district. But both are eager to accept DC dark money support in order to promote corporate PAC interests over the needs of our district.**

To solve the problems we face collectively, we need a clear-eyed, passionate leader -- one who grew up in our district, worked all over the world in service to our country, and came back home to activate communities neglected by our representation and build a grassroots network of people ready for change.

I believe that it’s time for TX-22 to have a leader -- one that will fight for our community, not for their own political gain. [**I’m humbled to be that candidate, and I’m honored to ask for your support.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17819723/207587875/2147176479?refcode=em_fr_2020.06.10_SPK_b1_all&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjk1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZWIzMzhkNy01ZWFiLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DxAZ7M33yLY1UyMTPrmVDiWAW2qpAyI6E5lQURZRN9w=)

Thank you,
Sri Preston Kulkarni
[DONATE ➜](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17819724/207587876/1700156400?refcode=em_fr_2020.06.10_SPK_b1_all&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjk1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZWIzMzhkNy01ZWFiLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DxAZ7M33yLY1UyMTPrmVDiWAW2qpAyI6E5lQURZRN9w=)
---

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17819725/207587877/1700156400?refcode=em_fr_2020.06.10_SPK_b1_all&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjk1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZWIzMzhkNy01ZWFiLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DxAZ7M33yLY1UyMTPrmVDiWAW2qpAyI6E5lQURZRN9w=)

**Sri Preston Kulkarni** is a lifelong public servant and national security expert who has spent his career overseas working to protect Americans here at home. Growing up in Houston, Sri personally experienced the same challenges that our communities are facing today. In Congress, he’ll fight to lower the cost of prescription drugs, stand up to the gun lobby, address climate change, and bring common-sense solutions to Washington. | [Help send Sri to Congress! ➜](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17819726/207587878/1700156400?refcode=em_fr_2020.06.10_SPK_b1_all&amount=15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjk1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZWIzMzhkNy01ZWFiLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DxAZ7M33yLY1UyMTPrmVDiWAW2qpAyI6E5lQURZRN9w=)
---
* * *
Bringing true representation to TX-22. Learn more at [sri2020.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17819727/207587879/307941387?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjk1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZWIzMzhkNy01ZWFiLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DxAZ7M33yLY1UyMTPrmVDiWAW2qpAyI6E5lQURZRN9w=&emci=7c802daa-53ab-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=7eb338d7-5eab-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=14145524)

name: Trump 100 Club
subject: Exclusive new club

name: Jim Oberweis
subject: Thank you for signing up!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jim 2020 Committee
body:
[ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/b49153ac4d1237a53a89ac0c84d83476/34533/cf7a33239bf7ec14/84b78d98f53b7953c3d6b838967d376c/34533) Peter,
Thank you for signing up to receive email updates from me and my campaign team. I’m grateful to have you on board!
As you may know, I am a businessman and lifelong Illinoisan who has fought for our Midwestern values in the State Senate, and now I want to take that same fight to Washington to bring fiscal sanity back to the swamp.
I’ve been a successful businessman because of the same principles that made me successful in Springfield, where I never spent my entire budget allotment and worked hard, sharing just one staffer with other legislators to cut costs.
More than that, I am a strongly rooted in our shared Midwestern values who loves our great country... **I’m running for this seat to stop the socialists, help rebuild our economy and improve lives for ALL Illinois residents!**
My life has been the embodiment of the America Dream. And as a family man, I am determined to do my part to help preserve that dream for future generations.
As a subscriber to my email list, you’ll receive regular updates as we move forward, and have opportunities to share your opinion and get more involved.
To make sure our emails reach your inbox, take this one quick action today: _Add**[info@jim2020.com](mailto:info@jim2020.com "info@jim2020.com")** to your email address book, contacts list, or safe senders list. This will help keep our emails out of your “Junk” or “Spam” folder_. You can also learn more about who I am and what I stand for on my **[campaign website](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/b49153ac4d1237a53a89ac0c84d83476/34533/cf7a33239bf7ec14/a6e206740136a9bcc3d6b838967d376c/34533)**.
Thank you again for signing up. We’ll be in touch soon!
Sincerely, [ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/b49153ac4d1237a53a89ac0c84d83476/34533/cf7a33239bf7ec14/e7495547c0e20f36c3d6b838967d376c/34533) Jim Oberweis
_P.S. If you believe you’ve received this email message in error - or do not wish to receive future updates from me or my team for any reason - you can[click here to unsubscribe](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/unsubscribe1.cfm?eid=b49153ac4d1237a53a89ac0c84d83476&c=34533&jid=cf7a33239bf7ec14)._ | | [](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/b49153ac4d1237a53a89ac0c84d83476/34533/cf7a33239bf7ec14/b34e065d86a07876c3d6b838967d376c/34533)

clean_body: Peter, Thank you for signing up to receive email updates from me and my campaign team. I’m grateful to have you on board! As you may know, I am a businessman and lifelong Illinoisan who has fought for our Midwestern values in the State Senate, and now I want to take that same fight to Washington to bring fiscal sanity back to the swamp. I’ve been a successful businessman because of the same principles that made me successful in Springfield, where I never spent my entire budget allotment and worked hard, sharing just one staffer with other legislators to cut costs. More than that, I am a strongly rooted in our shared Midwestern values who loves our great country... I’m running for this seat to stop the socialists, help rebuild our economy and improve lives for ALL Illinois residents! My life has been the embodiment of the America Dream. And as a family man, I am determined to do my part to help preserve that dream for future generations. As a subscriber to my email list, you’ll receive regular updates as we move forward, and have opportunities to share your opinion and get more involved. To make sure our emails reach your inbox, take this one quick action today: Addmailto:info@jim2020.com "info@jim2020.com" to your email address book, contacts list, or safe senders list. This will help keep our emails out of your “Junk” or “Spam” folder. You can also learn more about who I am and what I stand for on my . Thank you again for signing up. We’ll be in touch soon! Sincerely, Jim Oberweis P.S. If you believe you’ve received this email message in error - or do not wish to receive future updates from me or my team for any reason - you can. | | Paid for by Jim 2020 Committee

name: Team Cotton
subject: McConnell's take on Cotton and the Times
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cotton for Senate 2226 Cottondale Lane
body:
We wanted you to see Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell's remarks on Tom's takedown of the New York Times.Thanks for your continued support.Click here to unsubscribe http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/servlet/OneClickOptOutServlet?m=42699705&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc4MDg3MTI3MwS2&mt=1&rt=0
http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI2OTk3MDUS1&kn=12&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=2&j=MTc4MDg3MTI3MwS2&mt=1&rt=0 Peter,We wanted you to see Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell's remarks on Tom's takedown of the New York Times. “But one week ago, the Grey Lady finally met her match. Vladimir Putin? No problem. Iranian propaganda? Sure. But nothing could have prepared them for 800 words from the junior Senator for Arkansas."Thanks for your continued support.

-Team Cotton STAND FOR FREEDOM http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI2OTk3MDUS1&kn=8&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=2&j=MTc4MDg3MTI3MwS2&mt=1&rt=0 America Cannot Be America if Civil Disagreement Becomes a Contradiction in TermsSenate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI2OTk3MDUS1&kn=9&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=2&j=MTc4MDg3MTI3MwS2&mt=1&rt=0 Yesterday, I explained we cannot let the First Amendment become another casualty of this troubled moment.

“No matter how charged the issue, peaceful protests must be protected – from suppression by governments or hijackings by violent mobs. In the United States of America, people get to protest.

“In our country, people also get to worship.

As I explained yesterday, local officials cannot selectively enforce health restrictions to privilege some First Amendment gatherings over others. If mayors are posing for photographs in massive demonstrations, there is no reason why small, careful church services should stay banned.

These are formal constitutional questions. But our American culture of free expression and open debate is not only threatened from the top down, by the government. It can also dry up from beneath.

If we are to maintain the civic discourse that has made us great, American citizens and American institutions need to want it.

“In the last several years, the New York Times has published op-eds from Vladimir Putin, the foreign minister of Iran, and a leader of the Muslim Brotherhood. They have published an essay arguing for greater normalization of pedophilia.

As far as I know, none of those decisions occasioned public revolts from the paper’s staff, hand-wringing apologies from the editors, or an overhaul of the masthead. Presumably it was understood that pushing the envelope and airing disagreements are necessary in a free market of ideas.

But one week ago, the Grey Lady finally met her match.

Vladimir Putin? No problem. Iranian propaganda? Sure. But nothing could have prepared them for 800 words from the junior Senator for Arkansas.

“Senator Cotton wrote an op-ed explaining a position which one survey found 58 percent of Americans agreed with. He argued that leadership in several cities had proven they either couldn’t or wouldn’t stop the riots, so President Trump should use federal troops to secure the peace, as several presidents have in our history.

His view was controversial, no question. But there is also no question it was a legitimate view for a Senator to express.

Looting and arson were crippling cities nightly. Some local authorities seemed to be functionally sacrificing their cities’ small businesses to appease the mob. In Chicago, we’ve since learned, even Democratic aldermen were literally crying and pleading with their Democratic mayor to do something. So a United States Senator wrote about it.

Immediately, his idea met strong criticism. Now, that ought to be par for the course. In a free and open society, speech begets speech. Arguments beget counterarguments. We discuss and debate as fellow citizens.

But that is not quite what happened. Instead of trying to win the argument, the far left tried to end the discussion.

By now, we all know the routine. We’ve seen this movie before.

Rather than actually rebut speech, the far left instead tries to silence the speaker with a mixture of misrepresentations, sanctimonious moralizing, and bizarre, emotional word salads that nobody else could have standing to question.

This silencing tactic has escaped from the ivory tower and is spreading throughout American life. It sounds like a Mad Libs mixture between a therapy session and a university’s H.R. department.

So, sure enough, instead of attempting to defeat Senator Cotton’s ideas, the left set out to ban him from polite society.

Some New York Times employees flooded social media to claim their bosses had risked reporters’ physical safety with the Senator’s scary words. Outside leftists blasted the paper for airing the argument.

The Times itself began lying about what Senator Cotton had said. The paper’s own Twitter account has claimed he’d called for a crackdown on peaceful protests, when he specifically distinguished them from violent rioters.

One of the Times’ own opinion writers devoted her own column the next day to calling his view “fascist” and proclaiming him outside “the bounds of legitimate debate.”

Remember, this is a sitting Senator discussing a proposition that had majority support from the American people discussing a power that Congress gave to presidents 213 years ago and which presidents in the past have exercised.

But the facts couldn’t hold a candle to the hurt feelings. The New York Times had erred grievously by making people confront a different viewpoint. They had to atone.

When the dust settled, the top opinion editor was gone. His deputy -- reassigned. The piece was pulled out of the print edition. And a wandering, multi-paragraph apology now precedes it online. We’re talking about the New York Times.

I understand the new editor has made it clear that staff should notify her immediately if any published opinion makes them uncomfortable.

One of our nation’s most storied newspapers just had its intellectual independence challenged by an angry mob -- and they folded like a house of cards.

A jury of people on Twitter indicted them as accessories to a thought crime, and instead of telling them to go take a hike, the paper pleaded guilty and begged for mercy. Their readers’ comfortable bubble was reinflated. Their safe space was safe again.

Now, our colleague from Arkansas has a unique job. The far left cannot write angry e-mails to a university president or a publisher to get him fired. He cannot be silenced by professions of outrage or the use of magic words like “problematic.” His only bosses are his constituents.

But this broader left-wing obsession with banning heretics from the public square will be poison for this country if it persists.

Our Republic can survive a pandemic. It can survive civil unrest. But ideas and deliberation are our foundation. America cannot be America if civil disagreement becomes a contradiction in terms.

The liberal tradition in this country used to pride itself on being broad-minded.

But we have spent years watching major universities slowly exchange debate for uniformity, and rigor for psychological comfort. Now we see the free press repeating that error.

Let’s hope we look back on this as a silly anomaly and not a sad turning point for our democracy.”
Paid for by Cotton for Senate 2226 Cottondale Lane
Suite 200
Little Rock, AR 72202

Senator Cotton was honorably discharged from the U.S. Army. Images do not imply endorsement by the Department of Defense or any service branch.

They have published an essay arguing for greater normalization of pedophilia. As far as I know, none of those decisions occasioned public revolts from the paper’s staff, hand-wringing apologies from the editors, or an overhaul of the masthead. Presumably it was understood that pushing the envelope and airing disagreements are necessary in a free market of ideas. But one week ago, the Grey Lady finally met her match. Vladimir Putin? No problem. Iranian propaganda? Sure. But nothing could have prepared them for 800 words from the junior Senator for Arkansas. “Senator Cotton wrote an op-ed explaining a position which one survey found 58 percent of Americans agreed with. He argued that leadership in several cities had proven they either couldn’t or wouldn’t stop the riots, so President Trump should use federal troops to secure the peace, as several presidents have in our history. His view was controversial, no question. But there is also no question it was a legitimate view for a Senator to express. Looting and arson were crippling cities nightly. Some local authorities seemed to be functionally sacrificing their cities’ small businesses to appease the mob. In Chicago, we’ve since learned, even Democratic aldermen were literally crying and pleading with their Democratic mayor to do something. So a United States Senator wrote about it. Immediately, his idea met strong criticism. Now, that ought to be par for the course. In a free and open society, speech begets speech. Arguments beget counterarguments. We discuss and debate as fellow citizens. But that is not quite what happened. Instead of trying to win the argument, the far left tried to end the discussion. By now, we all know the routine. We’ve seen this movie before. Rather than actually rebut speech, the far left instead tries to silence the speaker with a mixture of misrepresentations, sanctimonious moralizing, and bizarre, emotional word salads that nobody else could have standing to question. This silencing tactic has escaped from the ivory tower and is spreading throughout American life. It sounds like a Mad Libs mixture between a therapy session and a university’s H.R. department. So, sure enough, instead of attempting to defeat Senator Cotton’s ideas, the left set out to ban him from polite society. Some New York Times employees flooded social media to claim their bosses had risked reporters’ physical safety with the Senator’s scary words. Outside leftists blasted the paper for airing the argument. The Times itself began lying about what Senator Cotton had said. The paper’s own Twitter account has claimed he’d called for a crackdown on peaceful protests, when he specifically distinguished them from violent rioters. One of the Times’ own opinion writers devoted her own column the next day to calling his view “fascist” and proclaiming him outside “the bounds of legitimate debate.” Remember, this is a sitting Senator discussing a proposition that had majority support from the American people discussing a power that Congress gave to presidents 213 years ago and which presidents in the past have exercised. But the facts couldn’t hold a candle to the hurt feelings. The New York Times had erred grievously by making people confront a different viewpoint. They had to atone. When the dust settled, the top opinion editor was gone. His deputy -- reassigned. The piece was pulled out of the print edition. And a wandering, multi-paragraph apology now precedes it online. We’re talking about the New York Times. I understand the new editor has made it clear that staff should notify her immediately if any published opinion makes them uncomfortable. One of our nation’s most storied newspapers just had its intellectual independence challenged by an angry mob -- and they folded like a house of cards. A jury of people on Twitter indicted them as accessories to a thought crime, and instead of telling them to go take a hike, the paper pleaded guilty and begged for mercy. Their readers’ comfortable bubble was reinflated. Their safe space was safe again. Now, our colleague from Arkansas has a unique job. The far left cannot write angry e-mails to a university president or a publisher to get him fired. He cannot be silenced by professions of outrage or the use of magic words like “problematic.” His only bosses are his constituents. But this broader left-wing obsession with banning heretics from the public square will be poison for this country if it persists. Our Republic can survive a pandemic. It can survive civil unrest. But ideas and deliberation are our foundation. America cannot be America if civil disagreement becomes a contradiction in terms. The liberal tradition in this country used to pride itself on being broad-minded. But we have spent years watching major universities slowly exchange debate for uniformity, and rigor for psychological comfort. Now we see the free press repeating that error. Let’s hope we look back on this as a silly anomaly and not a sad turning point for our democracy.” Paid for by Cotton for Senate 2226 Cottondale Lane Suite 200 Little Rock, AR 72202 Senator Cotton was honorably discharged from the U.S. Army. Images do not imply endorsement by the Department of Defense or any service branch.

body:

Team, Cory is hosting a virtual town hall tomorrow, **Thursday, June 11th** at **6:30 p.m. ET** to discuss his work in the Senate to address systemic racism and to reform policing. **[To dial into the town hall or join by video conference, you must RSVP in advance. Barbara Bollier is the leader we need serving Kansas in the U.S. Senate. Barbara will be a breath of fresh air in Washington and will get things done for our state. She has always stayed true to her values and will always put Kansans first.

|
---

| | I know a thing or two about leadership, team. I worked closely with President Barack Obama as Secretary of Health and Human Services. I also served as the governor of Kansas, leading our state during two terms in office. [**With this in mind, I can confidently tell you -- Barbara Bollier is the leader we need serving Kansas in the U.S. Senate. We can’t let them do it. Too many families are counting on us. [**If we stick together, we can win this battleground seat and get our country moving in the right direction again. name: Senator Patty Murray
subject: NEW POLL: Amy McGrath is in the lead
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY PEOPLE FOR PATTY MURRAY
body:
#

Team,
We just got some big news about the Kentucky Senate race:
**A new poll out yesterday shows that Democrat Amy McGrath just took the lead over Mitch McConnell. This is the clearest sign yet that this Senate seat is in play this November.**
**[Will you rush in a $5 contribution now to help Amy defeat Mitch in Kentucky?](https://act.pattymurray.?g=KnQfVcQot1BdNp6fCr3xM1A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pm-200609-mcgrath?refcode=em200609i&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=98208&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Everett&state=WA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**
Republicans once thought this was a safe seat -- but with Amy taking a one-point lead, there is no denying that Democrats are within striking distance of removing McConnell from the Senate.
Mitch McConnell isn't just failing the nation as Majority Leader -- he's letting his entire state down by pushing for the GOP's extreme agenda instead of fighting for Kentucky families.
That's why we need to beat him at the polls this November. Amy McGrath, a former Marine fighter pilot, is up for the challenge -- but she needs all of us to have her back in this fight.
**[Are you ready to defeat Mitch McConnell and take back the Senate? Make a $5 contribution now to support Amy McGrath in this crucial 2020 race.](https://act.pattymurray.?g=KnQfVcQot1BdNp6fCr3xM1A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pm-200609-mcgrath?refcode=em200609i&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=98208&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Everett&state=WA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**
Thank you,
Patty

**_As Washington's senior U.S. Senator, Patty Murray is working to help families, solve problems, and defend our progressive values._**
* * *

name: =?utf-8?Q?Jake=20LaTurner?=
subject: BREAKING: Kansans for Life endorsement
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kansans for LaTurner
body:
Fourth major endorsement for LaTurner for Congress | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/4cdfdd4a0d80/for-immediate-release-kansas-state-treasurer-jake-laturner-announces-candidacy-for-us-congress-6931526?e=1050d051da)
---
| | We are thrilled to announce another key endorsement for our campaign for Congress. Kansans for Life represents a community of devoted Kansans who know, as I do, that every life has value and we must do everything we can do protect the most vulnerable among us.

This is the fourth major endorsement our campaign has received from a known conservative organization, including Kansas Farm Bureau, National Association for Gun Rights, Family Policy Alliance, and now Kansans for Life.

[Would you consider chipping in to help us spread the good news? $100, $50, or even $15 helps. ](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=4cd8f3352b&e=1050d051da)
---
|
---
| | _I am incredibly humbled to receive their endorsement._ I have spent my entire life dedicated to the cause of protecting life, including during my time as a state senator when I introduced and helped pass Simon's Law, which ensured parents would be the ones in charge of health care decisions for their children.

Because of this, _**I was awarded the Pro-Life Hero Award. **_
---
|
---
| I will continue to be at the forefront of the fight for life at every stage and teach my children to do the same. It is our duty to protect life as the most precious gift that it is.

Yours in the fight,

Jake LaTurner
---
| [Donate to send a Pro-Life Conservative to Congress](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=cb6b24c777&e=1050d051da "Donate to send a Pro-Life Conservative to Congress")

name: Mark Warner for Senate
subject: No one should have to wait hours to vote.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
Long lines and broken machines
|
---
| | Derek --

Hours-long lines. Broken machines.

What happened in Georgia yesterday underscores the need to fix our broken elections system. We should be working hard to make sure it's easier for every American to vote. It's completely ridiculous that some folks had to wait in line for **_four hours to cast their ballots._**

Mark Warner is working with his Senate colleagues to fix our broken system and give every American fair access to cast their ballot come November through the _VoteSafe Act_.

The _VoteSafe Act_ aims to help people cast their ballots safely by:
* Requiring every state to allow all residents to vote by mail in all 2020 elections;
* Mandating that every state conduct early in-person voting for at least 20 days prior to all remaining 2020 elections; and
* Improving the voter registration process, to make sure that everyone is ready for Election Day.

**[Add your name as a citizen co-sponsor of this important bill.](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d45a/53d9897e/5482bdf7/1c887cd9/639604964/VEsH/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/s/warner-votesafeact")** Thanks to our General Assembly, Virginia already has expanded ballot access. Now, it's our responsibility to ensure that every American has the same opportunity to safely cast their ballot. We have the chance to make sure these protections are in place before the general election, but it's critical that we get the word out about this important bill.

Mark is committed to making sure everyone has the opportunity to cast their vote in November -- without having to make the choice between casting their ballot and protecting their health. **Will you help Mark raise public awareness of the _VoteSafe Act_ and become a citizen co-sponsor?** | **[Sign on as a citizen co-sponsor](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d45a/53d9897e/5482bdf7/1c887cd9/639604964/VEsE/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/s/warner-votesafeact")**
---
Thank you,
Team Warner

**[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d45a/53d9897e/5482bdf7/1c887cd8/639604964/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjYwNyJ9/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warner-votesafe?refcode=20200610_p_1_fl__607__&refcode2=cont&amount=10")**
---

|
---

Friends of Mark Warner
1490-5A Quarterpath Road #213
Williamsburg, VA 23185

[Unsubscribe](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d45a/53d9897e/5482bdf7/1c887cc7/639604964/VEsC/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://act.markwarnerva.com/unsubscribe")

subject: If it works in Arizona, it’ll work everywhere
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tom O'Halleran for Congress
body:
Even before a public health crisis made us rethink how to protect our democratic process, Arizona has ensured that every voter has their voice heard | | | [](http://links.tomohalleran.com/u/click?_t=63bcf327664141c1a59a283fcc863b63&_m=e266978700ca4d1db3e1482a7fde57d4&_e=QUTi4MOv_FffuIsp3GjUU_4j2DZcyHR7-1s63HAtwt3TP0MoZ6WdkW30l-J1LRjoD7E20aguNzLKRpQ_f6g1u9RIUdw1GmMxVopvMlf2RlhAXbbZnYQbd0geJkmn8Q68dP7KBQw5HZaMr9i4S0UyXmpRFn2yIQ-KMLaxYUM-WIy0XrRkT_BVEzqDMWtqjE3McnX0ekUPm8vHXmt8Ic4FPBNHDiE_dxI8-I4gzRr9eIomqebec9PV9RTQoTqLDLXuQAPJ7ltTE6rKzsC0Vgukrb31DCkEJyktU0Fha6SVyf1OqnukcA8tTFG61bHxFNC7) Arizona has shown the country that Vote by Mail is popular and it works. Even before a public health crisis made us rethink how to protect our democratic process, Arizona has ensured that every voter has their voice heard by making Vote by Mail easy to do and readily accessible. There’s a clear solution to protect Americans’ democracy during these challenging times: ensuring every voter can cast their ballot through the mail. **[I need you to add your name to this petition today to DEMAND that Congress protects our democracy by passing Vote by Mail for ALL.](http://links.tomohalleran.com/u/click?_t=63bcf327664141c1a59a283fcc863b63&_m=e266978700ca4d1db3e1482a7fde57d4&_e=QUTi4MOv_FffuIsp3GjUU_4j2DZcyHR7-1s63HAtwt3TP0MoZ6WdkW30l-J1LRjoD7E20aguNzLKRpQ_f6g1u9RIUdw1GmMxVopvMlf2RlgT0Z_CPuhCToP431Tr_hgAAZkO9S-54tq6VZNInuhOKRRJr6r4jEgNvusJMPYQolm7takTtg4Xl-nELZKjOVgibAL2nipbeC1QuN59oiUvJ-gdyqrhq9D8Wc1ztckt1exXhD_sn8wxFwEdIyLpWdXpQYmLJaIlk7-3hH7rBur_7OyqG9Mc3lOozQ-Rb7nT1zkl1bKBazWjrj1x4Nw_u0Vh)** [SIGN HERE](http://links.tomohalleran.com/u/click?_t=63bcf327664141c1a59a283fcc863b63&_m=e266978700ca4d1db3e1482a7fde57d4&_e=QUTi4MOv_FffuIsp3GjUU_4j2DZcyHR7-1s63HAtwt3TP0MoZ6WdkW30l-J1LRjoD7E20aguNzLKRpQ_f6g1u9RIUdw1GmMxVopvMlf2RlgKY0jcDV977B3JvDjzaN5zbOrl4LRbZ_1i-AxsGCDSlnCCJdfE51Jv-iVbi4EAJ2Y1eO5M4YtyEQnKs4W-lzldB1s59O5iWPWyiRAZ3mepC1Ihre1_lb12DMVSyVTgv7lwGxyLuioE4T2tSRHCEeHQ_LLK5r7rK9fJRcAIRl4vD-gjTzf4uZqz5zX-f5zes_rRixsJh3H8Wgf-7_oCiN71) Thank you,
Tom O’Halleran

name: Pete Buttigieg
subject: This election
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party
body:
| | | |
---|---
| | Greetings, fellow Hoosier Democrats. This is a painful and challenging time across America and here in Indiana. The nation is in upheaval as the anguish over institutional racism and police violence cries out for action, while we continue to see our daily lives radically transformed by the spread of a deadly pandemic. If one thing has become clearer than ever in these recent days, it’s that leadership matters. The issues we are grappling with go even beyond politics. But we have seen why it is deeply important who sits in the Oval Office, who legislates on Capitol Hill, and it is so extremely important who is in local and state offices. On Friday, Hoosier Democrats from around the state will come together for the [virtual Hoosier Hospitality Dinner](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17821672/207634061/-1853952297?emci=3043805b-aeaa-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=ea000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001&ceid=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMmJhYWY1YS03MmFiLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-wrqOch3WBpW790aDIVuRuECZkyXaxxr9EsUcyR8wD4=) and will pledge to delivering change in 2020. Right here in Indiana, we have nationally recognized battleground races and opportunities to take back key state and local offices. I hope you can join Hoosier Democrats to hear from keynote speaker Congresswoman Cheri Bustos, and I hope you'll join us as we fight to move Indiana forward. Stay well, stay involved and stay safe, Pete Buttigieg _Get your virtual Hoosier Hospitality Dinner tickets **[here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17821673/207634062/-1853952297?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMmJhYWY1YS03MmFiLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-wrqOch3WBpW790aDIVuRuECZkyXaxxr9EsUcyR8wD4=&emci=3043805b-aeaa-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=b2baaf5a-72ab-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=4925535)**. _ |

name: Luke Holland
subject: Confidential note from Inhofe Chief of Staff
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Jim Inhofe](https://click
body:
| |
---
As we speak, Senator Inhofe is chairing a Senate Armed Services Committee meeting where they are writing the National Defense Authorization Act. | [View in browser](https://click.ml.mailersend.com/link/c/YT0xNDQwNzU2NzQxOTg4OTQ3NTU1JmM9YTNjNCZlPTE5MjgmYj0zNTczNTM0MjQmZD1kOGQxcTVq.Hh2wQCe0n09RZKako8lKx4LmgQdTsG31wSuBvMioMBg)
| | |
---
|
---
| |
---
| Hello Friend - As we speak, Senator Inhofe is chairing a Senate Armed Services Committee meeting where they are writing the National Defense Authorization Act. It’s the biggest bill of the year and the most important because it makes sure our troops have the training and equipment they need to complete the mission and return home safe. It’s not an easy job. Our military was obliterated during the Obama administration. During his last five years in office, he cut defense spending by 25 percent, while China and Russia were beefing their militaries up. Sen. Inhofe knows the consequences of that -- and it’s one of the main reasons he’s running for reelection. You won’t see this hearing on TV because it is classified, but I wanted to drop you a note to let you know what he’s working on and how this bill gets us one step closer to President Trump and Senator Inhofe’s shared goal of rebuilding our military. Sometimes these meetings go well into the night, but as soon as I’m able, I’ll let you know how things go. The stakes are high. After the bill is approved by Sen. Inhofe’s committee and the full Senate, Inhofe will have to go toe to toe with Nancy Pelosi’s liberal House of Representatives to get them to agree with what we need to do to defend America--so his bill needs to be as strong as possible. Many liberal House Members would like nothing more than to cut defense spending like President Obama did to pay for social programs. But as Inhofe said before, “I’d stop that with my dying breath.”
Luke Holland

---
| | | Chief of Staff, Senator Jim Inhofe
---
---

Friend,

Do you have everything you need for Father’s Day?

We just launched our BRAND NEW Trump MAGA Dad Whiskey Glasses, and we want YOU to have a set.

name: Chris Jacobs
subject: The choice is clear
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JACOBS FOR CONGRESS [Unsubscribe](http://sendy
body:
|
---
Friend, Did you see the debate last night? Nate McMurray is unhinged, and his socialist proposals would raise taxes, make our cities and towns less safe by defunding and disarming police, and threaten the constitutional rights of every New Yorker. As I said last night, not only does Nate not share our values, he mocks them and insults them every single chance he gets. He will fight against our second amendment rights in Congress and believes that our pro-life values make us part of the "American Taliban." He even has gone so far as to say [Trump supporters are Traitors.](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/OAst8O6Rl3q4KZFnr763R83g/trTlMJq763rnHZ72paA5rwNQ/hDS9bj1iVRmRF763L4QXlCaw) Not only is this absurd, but his rhetoric is also dangerous and divisive. The only thing traitorous in my opinion is someone who is actively campaigning on destroying and eroding constitutional rights. That's why this election on June 23rd is so critical. I am proud to be the only candidate in NY27 to have the President's [complete and total endorsement](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/OAst8O6Rl3q4KZFnr763R83g/VWGdqdS763e2y60ToDn1UBIw/hDS9bj1iVRmRF763L4QXlCaw). When I go to Congress in two weeks I plan to fight alongside our President as he fights for all of us so we can usher in the great American comeback - that means getting tough on China, rebuilding our economy and supporting small business, and working toward our President's America First Agenda. [With less than two weeks to go until Election Day, can you help ensure we are successful by donating $5, $10, or $25 right now?](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/OAst8O6Rl3q4KZFnr763R83g/AHlIDqZjMPNLQWP82e763DkQ/hDS9bj1iVRmRF763L4QXlCaw) With me as your Congressman, your values will be protected, our Western New York way of life will be respected, and our future will be bright and prosperous. Thank you for your continued support. Best, Chris Jacobs [](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/OAst8O6Rl3q4KZFnr763R83g/JRonsCdsGcbXjao3MsNkqg/hDS9bj1iVRmRF763L4QXlCaw)
[](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/OAst8O6Rl3q4KZFnr763R83g/tEgLPl5T4lLCPX3O0ADHrw/hDS9bj1iVRmRF763L4QXlCaw) [](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/OAst8O6Rl3q4KZFnr763R83g/FRCWWrDX6pUTSC763pjuQ9ig/hDS9bj1iVRmRF763L4QXlCaw)
* * *

body:
Claudia helped make America great two years ago. Now Donald Trump needs her back in Congress to help us keep America great. [ ](http://links.claudiaforcongress.com/u/click?_t=e8d915629893476cb837806f005d02de&_m=9b15ed9800f8417cb0c8a05e51e6cb9a&_e=vKEtZoUrwa-5bwqqRVEz2UKB5p6NqH3AK-eHqRrzpYrRdL2dwybpDGdRkgyiS_d09z2Zk8zlAaA3iBQh62Rou47HIFUKKiXg5ga3Lu11x3yaabGNJskTWWRUskVmkJvr_akyfSP3IvoJOqAh3KhGlZU0cAXHFjTAl7m1rAVWFcMwGK1fi6VhfCSTLP5m3YDny3XYP0SAruHGlnzmjqTQHGRQNCgj-0mTZOwgiPCzxjqRMLaH38OQpGnNPMz7SsXmdO3RCoQK-Aek8OYzs2Z_fZ41mGZDhsX35diRGiIWS5ugxnjZ_AF95V_3ugN70XCqS60R2n_OPFxv_nYuQWDm3XjBVN_3phKZ_ZR93elYfshGhh9iS8Nz4awtO_zK8o87YOcLck1iz8FHz_kYuDtgfHXj6DdTut9NU_S0N4v1n7n9w_o_jTajgSpNCbu8KqfXJfF1R73F-3T2b59KoDNKMCXzkXqr8eK98GWwm1AE1TU%3D) Friend, Yesterday, I joined a virtual meet-and-greet with Claudia and her supporters, and I have to admit – I couldn’t possibly be any more impressed with Claudia as a candidate.

**Today, our President is working on the Great American Comeback, and we need to pack Washington full of conservatives like Claudia this November to make this as successful as possible.**
Claudia is indispensable to my father’s agenda. That’s why I’m going out of my way to help defeat liberal Anthony Brindisi who consistently sides with Nancy Pelosi instead of his constituents in Upstate New York.

name: MJ Hegar
subject: A real shot to flip Texas
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
Texas has so much untapped potential, but it's going to take your help to win in November.

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648901ff/7f51f7bc/4ef81e51/4ef6f60b/2800468976/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | We've got a real shot at flipping Texas, Peter, and here's why:
* **Despite being in his seat for 18 years, Sen. Cornyn has spent his career serving the special interests, so he has abysmal approval ratings -- 37% -- in Texas!**
* **34% of Texas voters are still undecided ** about who they're going to support in November -- that's a lot of voters we can reach.

* As a combat veteran and working mom, MJ is the Texas tough candidate who can WIN this state.

name: Teresa Tomlinson
subject: While our campaign is ending, the effort to flip Georgia blue and take back the Senate is just beginning
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TERESA TOMLINSON FOR SENATE
body:
| |
---

| | **** | | Friend,

Not long ago we received word that the Democratic primary election for the U.S. Senate in Georgia had been decided. The voters have spoken. Unfortunately, we came up short.

The fact that we were the runner-up campaign is thanks to the support we have received from those of you across Georgia and the entire country, and for that I am truly grateful to each and every one of you.

Our job now turns to supporting the Democratic nominee and doing everything we can to replace David Perdue, win the White House, and flip the Senate majority. I vow to do my part to help realize the historic opportunity we have to win Georgia for the Democrats and ask you to join me in supporting our nominee.

Grateful for you,

Teresa Tomlinson

---

* * *

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: JUST IN: More good news on the U.S. comeback
body:
Press Secretary Kayleigh McEnany just shared some terrific news about our nation’s fight to slow the spread of Coronavirus. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=492cfb4c23&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • June 10, 2020
---
| | |
# **JUST IN: More good news on the U.S. comeback**

**Press Secretary Kayleigh McEnany** just shared some terrific news about our nation’s fight to slow the spread of Coronavirus.

**According to Dr. Birx** , a new study suggests that the United States prevented more than _3 million_ Coronavirus deaths thanks to aggressive mitigation efforts by President Trump and other officials. Those efforts include early travel restrictions with China, national public health guidelines, rapid testing and ventilator production, and more.

**Another study found** that these national shutdown efforts prevented 50 million additional U.S. COVID-19 cases, as well.

**Thanks to the selfless** patriotism of workers all across our country who did their part, America’s economy is now reopening safely, strongly, and well ahead of schedule.

[**Pundits were stunned**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=aecf8bc037&e=3ee4985eea) on Friday, for example, when they first saw the monthly jobs report for May. After “experts” projected that the United States would lose 7.5 million jobs last month, the economy added 2.5 million—the biggest monthly gain _in history_.

**🎬** [Larry Kudlow: America is coming back!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=23ae90538f&e=3ee4985eea)

**“It was, as economist** Chris Rupkey emailed, the ‘biggest forecast miss of our life,’” _The Washington Post_ reported. “What the heck happened? In short, give some credit to the government relief efforts, especially the Paycheck Protection Program, for bringing back jobs.”

**The Paycheck Protection Program** , signed into law by President Trump, provides forgivable loans to businesses that kept their employees on payroll during the pandemic.

**In fairness** to the media, they are used to the slow economic “recoveries” of the past, such as the weak growth seen under former President Obama. But the Trump Economy is different, bolstered by pro-growth, pro-worker policies that created wealth broadly, cut down income inequality, and dropped unemployment to its lowest rate in 50 years.

**“Because the patient** was strong in the beginning, because [of] the policies that [President Trump] put into place to cut taxes, roll back regulation, unleash American energy, fight for free and fair trade, we created more than 7 million jobs” before the Coronavirus struck, Vice President Mike Pence says. Now, we’re rebounding quickly.

**🎬****VP Pence:** [Credit President Trump’s leadership during this pandemic](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ba066f21f9&e=3ee4985eea)

**“The greatest comeback** in American history,” President Trump called it on Friday, delivering remarks from the White House Rose Garden. “It’s not going to stop here.”

**As America reopens** , President Trump and the Coronavirus Task Force continue to lead a nationwide response to keep the virus under control.

**“The best strategy** to ensure the health of our people moving forward is to focus our resources on protecting high-risk populations—like the elderly and those in nursing homes—while allowing younger and healthy Americans to get back to work immediately and open up our schools,” the President said Friday.

**Another economic milestone** came today: For the third day in a row, the NASDAQ stock index closed at an all-time high. “Tremendous progress being made, way ahead of schedule,” President Trump tweeted this morning. “USA!”

[“Trump Signs New Law Relaxing PPP Rules: What You Need to Know”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3f9b558cfe&e=3ee4985eea)

**🎬****MORE** : [Press Secretary delivers statement on renaming military bases](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7ef344abd1&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## Photo of the Day
---
|
---
| **President Trump signs H.R. 7010, the “PPP Flexibility Act of 2020”** | June 5, 2020
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c2e5589317&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c97ebffb1c&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=c8ff06aadf)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

name: Holcomb Crew
subject: One Indiana - the great outdoors
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
The first official day of summer may not be until June 20 – but our state is starting to reopen and Hoosiers are ready to enjoy the outdoors. Friend, The first official day of summer may not be until June 20 – **but our state is starting to reopen and Hoosiers are ready to enjoy the outdoors.** With temperatures rising, the sun staying out longer, and the state safely progressing through Eric’s Back on Track Plan, it’s the perfect time to get out and enjoy this beautiful Indiana weather. And while some activities may look different this year, there are still lots of great opportunities to get outside. Our team pulled together a list of our favorite summer activities, and we hope they inspire you to get outside and enjoy our beautiful state.
* **Grilling out or lighting a bonfire** – Food never tastes better than when cooked over an open flame!
* **Catching fireflies** – Did you know that in 2018, Governor Holcomb signed into law making the Say's Firefly the Indiana state insect? So that means it’s time to get outside and light up your evening!
* **Gardening** – Do you have a green thumb? Consider growing a peony, the Indiana state flower!
* **Home repairs** – With this great weather, why not build some equity into your home with a little light construction?
* **Exercise!** – Feeling stressed or overwhelmed? Exercise! Head outside and find your nearest trail for running, walking or biking!
* **Go camping** – Indiana campgrounds are now open! Take a home away from home trip and head to your favorite Indiana campground!
* **Go for a round of golf** – Indiana is fortunate to have been home to one of the most legendary golf course designers in the world, Pete Dye. He designed seven golf courses in Indiana, which comprise the Pete Dye Golf Trail. **[Check it out!](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=41b5b9bc768b418495d8b16cee10db7c&_e=BCSE2xWhfWkFNEDI0vaWjOcbIloFjTAkez--gW2RMBdxOAjjlc7UINaUe4PuIgroARSRZhaFUaUh-DVplH9J4uERTgnixpVN3fptlJ7SHc_ktnRBFBBiBkx48J8oDqBReXyAI8B9SyAeBhiczaCpRT-C-PAi9zm0pTvYhCTFlWtzK89hgeKvB2cqVAOobq0DRNS3xZUeQJ8RsxpF3eLmmg_AfIbfLbTbx4SC8MRSk3mBLOZThPgtC64U5awZwhLac6jjn4aleGXp2t4jgcLLrxpIsXrl6Uphc6hrVhPdnQgXyRUd7aA1s2p-eK36a-QX1EP6_GnXRiw2baSACvbi1zauwx7-sxrkWhQrdxEQGngcEZ-i1Pv8cHn4LU1yD97qxZ-Qb_NcGlWvij3_g2GmDA%3D%3D)**
* **Water fun** – While some local public pools are closed this year, you can still cool off by turning the sprinklers on, or making a slip-n-slide at home. Or with Holiday World in Southern Indiana opening next week, right now their waterpark – Splashin’ Safari – is scheduled to open in July.
* **Go on a road trip** – You don’t have to go far to find a new, exciting place! As Indiana continues our phased reopening, **[find a place here in Indiana you’ve never visited](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=41b5b9bc768b418495d8b16cee10db7c&_e=BCSE2xWhfWkFNEDI0vaWjLc_wpJElRjdu8PiJIm8fQTIkKNDEzf2K-kqSH9ZfklN8-wBxMddJ69CvccE2DY4-M9RHid4uvFJQCFV0mhFjZhmovcegNq19fAwrvE73FUI4RuSlbsVpj3tOQ7qtk-XsfKGv2sBzh_TO8ggwXzU0tP-vTXWpufikjXGQLQp9KnpHQKCXt-wG8YE5qabbUfyPCL4CFPQkWE6USYDlpYHr2T-AaBvWCKuTJIkBQrDOVBUoWpD7J1oXjbL4UKBJsTL5TVRXasP65aobgIknTYmggPsNWrUVsdPTylZEzbJxiyw5IPwIw_i7pb5961gc93EEgmI16cZ-wZznior06dbzq2f5lQ-7v4LvTQ827gLS2jA)** and start planning your destination!
* **Visit an Indiana State Park** – Indiana State Park Inns are now open, and our state parks are ready to welcome you for a daytrip (or longer)! Have you tried the **[Three Dunes Challenge](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=41b5b9bc768b418495d8b16cee10db7c&_e=BCSE2xWhfWkFNEDI0vaWjDXn_WLcBxWeWi3huz20jEW-Pr8SCsbT8hyMiyrCX7XEcfBwDQkdtb9m_BO0W8bY7QVQg_FYy9w_gsYYQJQI0IxEcxE0OZkRp9-iWJZ3E-HjqfgLPCWRlTDzA7_tXtxwmqInjK7MKgJFE0HS4IyFX9BGnF2nQ5eqdyGXEcGhityf2gX8cP-mxpk3UfAJJq0mCbCoQK-K0DT5T8lEUVQbKRdRQDZBESKM02H-T4x63JkR0n1e_YGC4uLxymqzf7Pjox-bjVOcrJ-Yhtl1otoMQ4b1MYT5_KxEzqiuZi-jbDIFF-RxeSfRSPdTpZym_Ve-782jfnG9cgbEWoTnBkmzW6rNKNsYAqWyLL3x1KIwKTfpkYcLpumpAGJ86wK8hefnihU-2-I6pDAPQalITZsv-hw%3D)** at Indiana Dunes?
* **Check out a drive-in movie** – There’s no better way to be outdoors, practice social distancing, and enjoy time with friends and family than at the drive-in!

There are lots of ways to get outside and enjoy this incredible weather and make the most out of summer. However, remember – let’s be safe, and continue to practice safe social distancing as we go out.
Happy summer! -Your Holcomb Crew |

clean_body: The first official day of summer may not be until June 20 – but our state is starting to reopen and Hoosiers are ready to enjoy the outdoors. Friend, The first official day of summer may not be until June 20 – but our state is starting to reopen and Hoosiers are ready to enjoy the outdoors. With temperatures rising, the sun staying out longer, and the state safely progressing through Eric’s Back on Track Plan, it’s the perfect time to get out and enjoy this beautiful Indiana weather. And while some activities may look different this year, there are still lots of great opportunities to get outside. Our team pulled together a list of our favorite summer activities, and we hope they inspire you to get outside and enjoy our beautiful state. Grilling out or lighting a bonfire – Food never tastes better than when cooked over an open flame! Catching fireflies – Did you know that in 2018, Governor Holcomb signed into law making the Say's Firefly the Indiana state insect? So that means it’s time to get outside and light up your evening! Gardening – Do you have a green thumb? Consider growing a peony, the Indiana state flower! Home repairs – With this great weather, why not build some equity into your home with a little light construction? Exercise! – Feeling stressed or overwhelmed? Exercise! Head outside and find your nearest trail for running, walking or biking! Go camping – Indiana campgrounds are now open! Take a home away from home trip and head to your favorite Indiana campground! Go for a round of golf – Indiana is fortunate to have been home to one of the most legendary golf course designers in the world, Pete Dye. He designed seven golf courses in Indiana, which comprise the Pete Dye Golf Trail. Water fun – While some local public pools are closed this year, you can still cool off by turning the sprinklers on, or making a slip-n-slide at home. Or with Holiday World in Southern Indiana opening next week, right now their waterpark – Splashin’ Safari – is scheduled to open in July. Go on a road trip – You don’t have to go far to find a new, exciting place! As Indiana continues our phased reopening, and start planning your destination! Visit an Indiana State Park – Indiana State Park Inns are now open, and our state parks are ready to welcome you for a daytrip (or longer)! Have you tried the at Indiana Dunes? Check out a drive-in movie** – There’s no better way to be outdoors, practice social distancing, and enjoy time with friends and family than at the drive-in! There are lots of ways to get outside and enjoy this incredible weather and make the most out of summer. However, remember – let’s be safe, and continue to practice safe social distancing as we go out. Happy summer! -Your Holcomb Crew | | | | | | Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana. © Copyright 2020. All rights reserved. | | Eric Holcomb for Indiana, 101 W Ohio Street, Suite 2200, Indianapolis, IN 46204 | (317) 569-0709 | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com | | |

name: ChristyforCongress.org
subject: Equal pay for equal work
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress
body:
| []()
---

| | Peter --

It's 2020 -- the wage gap should not be something women have to worry about.

**But still, women's work is valued less than the equivalent work from a man.**

In 2020, women are paid about 82 cents for every dollar a man is. **And it's worse for Black women and Hispanic women,** who on average are paid 62 cents and 54 cents, respectively, for their work compared to a man's $1.

The Equal Pay Act was passed 57 years ago -- **and it's time for us to do something to actually honor the mission of the act.**

In Congress, Christy would advocate for enforcement measures and accountability regarding the wage gap to make sure every worker is paid the same wage for the same work, regardless of their gender identity, race, religion, sexual orientation, age, or any other difference.

**[Equal pay for equal work should not be a question or a debate any longer -- it is a right we need and deserve. If you agree, add your name to Christy's petition calling for the enforcement of the Equal Pay Act now>> ](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6ba7a/29776869/9af9388/569f32a5/916978855/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA2MTBfZXF1YWxwYXktZnVsbCJ9/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://christysmith.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/2006_email_smith_equalpay?source=em20_200610_equalpay-full")** [ADD YOUR NAME](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6ba7a/29776869/9af9388/569f32a5/916978855/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA2MTBfZXF1YWxwYXktZnVsbCJ9/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://christysmith.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/2006_email_smith_equalpay?source=em20_200610_equalpay-full") That the wage gap still exists in 2020 is unacceptable -- and we must work hard now to make sure future generations of workers do not receive different wages for the equivalent work.

That this is even a discussion we have to have is ridiculous. **It's time to do right by millions of women and close the wage gap once and for all.**

**[Sign on to Christy's petition calling for the closing of the wage gap. It's time.](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6ba7a/29776869/9af9388/569f32a5/916978855/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA2MTBfZXF1YWxwYXktZnVsbCJ9/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://christysmith.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/2006_email_smith_equalpay?source=em20_200610_equalpay-full")**

Thanks for stepping up and taking action.

\-- Team Christy

| Christy Smith is a community leader from CA-25 who has fought for her neighbors on the school board and in the California state legislature. Now she needs our help to defeat Mike Garcia and flip this battleground House seat blue in November. Help elect a champion for our health care, our schools, and our rights.
[DONATE](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6ba7a/29776869/9af9388/569f32a6/916978855/VEsC/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA2MTBfZXF1YWxwYXktZnVsbCJ9/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2006_email_christy?refcode=em20_200610_equalpay-full")
